assumptions: The intragroup dividend has resulted in a foreign-currency-denominated monetary receivable/payable that is recognised for consolidation purposes.
The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9 and IAS 21.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised monetary receivable can fall within the recognised-item hedge family, which maps to the fair value hedge model under IFRS 9.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Foreign currency risk on a recognised item can also fall within the variability-in-cash-flows hedge family, which maps to the cash flow hedge model under IFRS 9.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 and IFRIC 16 identify a distinct hedge-accounting model for foreign currency risk associated with a net investment in a foreign operation.
